headlines.text: WHCD shooter Cole Allen arraigned in DC federal court as Trump calls him a 'sick guy'
Iran FM Araghchi meets Putin in St. Petersburg as US-Iran peace talks remain stuck
Rubio rejects Iran's Hormuz deal offer; IRGC says strait control is 'definitive strategy'
OpenAI and Microsoft overhaul deal: exclusivity ends, OpenAI can now use any cloud provider
Michael Jackson biopic opens to record-shattering $97M domestic, $217M globally
German Chancellor Merz says US has 'no strategy' in Iran and is being 'humiliated'
Gas prices tick up for 5th straight day to $4.11 national average, still below April 9 peak
Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon on Sunday, deadliest day since ceasefire extension
Iran's Hormuz blockade has cut tanker traffic to about 5% of pre-war daily average
US Navy orders ships to 'shoot and kill' any vessel laying mines in Strait of Hormuz

breaking.text: Cole Allen, suspected WHCD shooter, arraigned in federal court Monday in DC on charges of using a firearm during a crime of violence and assault on a federal officer.

stories.hed: Iran Offers Hormuz Deal, US Says No Thanks; Trump Suggests Tehran Can Just Call
stories.dek: Iran proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts its naval blockade — Secretary Rubio called it unacceptable before the ink was dry. Trump canceled his envoys' trip to Pakistan and suggested Iran could just pick up the phone, which is one way to run a war.

stories.hed: Israeli Strikes Kill 14 in Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Extension, Hezbollah Fires Drones
stories.dek: The US-brokered Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended three weeks ago is looking increasingly theoretical, with Sunday marking the deadliest day of the truce so far. At least 2,509 people have been killed in Lebanon by Israeli fire since early March.

stories.hed: WHCD Shooter Cole Allen Arraigned: 'Manifesto,' Anti-Admin Motive, and a Hotel Security Mess
stories.dek: Cole Allen, 31, a CalTech-trained engineer and part-time tutor from Torrance, CA, faces federal charges after breaching a checkpoint at the White House Correspondents' Dinner with a shotgun. A Secret Service agent's vest stopped one round; Trump got a third brush with gunfire — and immediately used it to argue for his White House ballroom.

stories.hed: Microsoft and OpenAI Break Up — Sort Of — In Landmark AI Deal Restructuring
stories.dek: Microsoft has agreed to drop its exclusive right to sell OpenAI's models, meaning ChatGPT can now live on Amazon, Google, and anyone else willing to host it. Microsoft stops paying revenue share to OpenAI; MSFT stock dipped on the news because apparently fewer strings attached is scarier than more.

stories.hed: Germany's Merz Says America Is Being 'Humiliated' by Iran — And He Might Have a Point
stories.dek: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz told students Monday that the US has 'no strategy' on Iran, noting that American envoys flew to Islamabad only to leave without results while Tehran keeps the Strait locked down. The Iranians, he added, are 'clearly stronger than one thought' — a diplomatic observation that is sure to play well in Mar-a-Lago.

markets.context: Stocks drifted lower as stalled Iran peace talks and a closed Strait of Hormuz kept oil elevated; Goldman Sachs raised its Brent forecast to $90/bbl by late 2026 and global inventories are drawing at a record pace.

gas.note: Gas prices have risen five straight days — because nothing says 'peace talks are going great' like paying more to fill your tank.

popCulture.hed: Michael Jackson Biopic 'Michael' Moonwalks to Record $97M Domestic Opening Weekend
popCulture.dek: Antoine Fuqua's MJ biopic starring nephew Jaafar Jackson opened to the biggest music biopic debut in history, pulling $217M globally despite a brutal 38% Rotten Tomatoes score. Critics called it sanitized; audiences gave it an A- on CinemaScore and danced in the aisles anyway — Bohemian Rhapsody arc speedrun.

genZ.definition: A phrase originally used sincerely to fact-check viral TikToks using the X/Grok AI chatbot, now weaponized as pure satire — slapped under obviously absurd claims, personal drama, and news headlines to mock how unhinged the internet has become. It exploded in April 2026 as a go-to way to process the sheer volume of unbelievable-but-real news.
genZ.usage: Trump cancels peace envoy trip, suggests Iran just call him on the phone. 'Grok, is this true?' — yes, tragically.

tldr: Day 59 of the US-Iran war saw Iran offer a Hormuz deal that the US swiftly rejected, while Trump suggested Tehran could just dial his number; back home, the man who fired a shotgun at the White House Correspondents' Dinner was arraigned in federal court and the Michael Jackson biopic made $97 million proving critics are optional.
